![mood-tracker-add-to-note-demo](https://github.com/dartungar/obsidian-mood-tracker/assets/36126057/6fdb3ee6-2075-496e-a007-eb01e028c5ed)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
✨ **Customize mood rating labels**
You can now customize mood rating labels!

Mood rating label is a text (emoji is a text, too!) that displays instead of mood rating (1-5) in tracking modal and in graphs.
![image](https://github.com/dartungar/obsidian-mood-tracker/assets/36126057/c9df2bc3-0e6f-4315-bd9b-a0272106a639)

These can be customized now (look in the settings!):
![image](https://github.com/dartungar/obsidian-mood-tracker/assets/36126057/91489dc5-c0b8-409e-99be-6e13612f8f90)
releases.name: 1.2.1: customize mood rating labels

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
✨**Embeddable history / stats block**

You can embed the graph into a note with codeblock:
```mood-tracker-stats
end: current-week
daysBeforeEnd: 90
showAverage: true
showMostCommonMood: true
showCommonEmotionsList: true
height: 500px
```

Options:
- end: date in "YYYY-MM-DD" format, or dynamic date ("today", "current-week" (end of), "previous-week" (end of), "current-month" (end of), "previous-month" (end of))
- daysBeforeEnd: number of days to show
- showAverage: show average mood rating (defaults to false)
- showMostCommonMood: show the most common emotion (defaults to false)
- showCommonEmotionsList: show a list of the most common emotions (defaults to false)
- height: height of the graph (pixels, %, anything that `style` attribute of HTML element would support) (defaults to 350px)
- width: the same as height (defaults to 100%)

For example, this codeblock will render records for the current month, with no additional info:

```mood-tracker-stats
end: current-month
daysBeforeEnd: 39
```
releases.name: 1.2.0: embeddable history / stats block

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ✨You can now decide if you want to use "emotions" at all via "Use emotions" setting. If turned off, you won't see Emotions section in Mood tracker modal or Settings.
releases.name: 1.0.1: You can now turn "emotions" off

releases.author: dartungar
releases.description:
🎊This release brings a lot of features!

✨You can now edit existing entries via "edit" icon in stats modal.
✨You can now add new entries for past days via "add new entry" button in stats modal.
✨Date & time of entry can be set manually in tracker modal. It defaults to current date & time.
✨You can now move entries data (`mood-tracker-data.json`) via settings.
✨Tracker modal title can be customized via settings.
🌈Style improvements.
releases.name: 1.0.0: Edit past entries, add entries for past days, and more

releases.author: dartungar
releases.description: ✨Added some simple data integrity checks to prevent loss of data entries
releases.name: 0.3.4: data integrity improvements

releases.author: dartungar
releases.description:
✨ Vertical scale on stats chart is now based on possible values
✨ Show emoji instead of number on vertical scale legend
releases.name: 0.3.3: chart improvements

releases.author: dartungar
releases.description:
🐞There were some cases when data could be lost, e.g:
1. You have some kind of sync solution
2. Plugin data has been synced, but to load actual mood tracked entries from file into memory you had to reload plugin
3. If you did not, and added a new entry via plugin, it would override synced data with old data (before sync) plus the new entry.

To remedy this (sorry!), mood tracker entries are loaded from file each time you open modal for adding new entries.
releases.name: 0.3.2: fix sync issue

releases.author: dartungar
releases.description: 🐞fixed a bug where date inputs in stats window would sometimes freeze the entire app
releases.name: 0.3.1: fixes for date inputs

releases.author: dartungar
releases.description: ✨When creating a mood tracker entry, you now have an option to also insert its content into current note (useful for journaling). Configure the template in settings!
releases.name: 0.3.0: insert an entry into current note
